candidates.title: Create a Polkadot Account Programmatically
candidates.notes: Complete tutorial with steps for JS/TS, Python, and Rust. Produces a new account with mnemonic, public key, and address. CI tested.

candidates.title: Query Account Information with SDKs
candidates.notes: Full tutorial with step-by-step instructions for 5 SDKs (PAPI, Polkadot.js, Dedot, Python, Subxt). CI tested.

candidates.title: Query On-Chain State with Sidecar REST API
candidates.notes: Guide showing curl commands to query balances, assets, and blocks via Sidecar REST API. Relies on external Sidecar instance.

candidates.title: Query On-Chain State with SDKs
candidates.notes: Complete tutorial with 5 SDKs showing how to query account balances and asset information. CI tested.

candidates.title: Call Runtime APIs with SDKs
candidates.notes: Complete tutorial demonstrating runtime API calls (AccountNonceApi, Metadata) across 5 SDKs. Includes reference table of available runtime APIs.

candidates.title: Calculate Transaction Fees on Polkadot
candidates.notes: Tutorial with concrete steps to estimate fees using PAPI and Polkadot.js API. Produces fee estimate output.

candidates.title: Replay and Dry Run XCMs Using Chopsticks
candidates.notes: Detailed tutorial with setup, chain forking, XCM replay, and dry-run scripts. Requires building Wasm runtime locally which adds complexity.

candidates.title: Estimate XCM Fees for Asset Teleport
candidates.notes: Comprehensive tutorial for estimating XCM teleport fees between Polkadot Hub and People Chain. Requires Chopsticks for local forks.

candidates.title: Transfer Assets from Ethereum into Polkadot via Snowbridge
candidates.notes: Complete end-to-end tutorial: project setup, token approval, bridge transfer execution, and monitoring. Uses ParaSpell SDK and Snowbridge.

candidates.title: Transfer Assets Between Parachains with ParaSpell
candidates.notes: Full tutorial: build XCM transfer, dry run, verify ED, get fee estimates, submit transaction. Uses ParaSpell SDK on Paseo TestNet.

candidates.title: Pay Transaction Fees with Alternative Tokens
candidates.notes: Tutorial covering 3 SDKs (PAPI, Polkadot.js, Subxt) to send DOT transfer while paying fees in USDT. Uses Chopsticks fork.

candidates.title: Send Transactions with SDKs
candidates.notes: Complete tutorial demonstrating balance transfer across 5 SDKs (PAPI, Polkadot.js, Dedot, Python, Subxt). CI tested.

candidates.title: Convert Assets on Asset Hub via AMM
candidates.notes: Tutorial using Polkadot.js Apps UI to create liquidity pools, add/remove liquidity, and swap assets. UI-based rather than programmatic.

candidates.title: Register a Foreign Asset on Polkadot Hub
candidates.notes: Tutorial with UI-based steps using Polkadot.js Apps. Involves XCM call construction and cross-chain registration. Requires Chopsticks multi-chain setup.

candidates.title: Register a Local Asset on Polkadot Hub
candidates.notes: Step-by-step UI tutorial using Polkadot.js Apps to register a local asset. Medium priority because it is UI-based (not programmatic).

candidates.title: Run a Block-Producing Collator for Polkadot System Parachains
candidates.notes: Complete end-to-end tutorial covering install, node key generation, chain spec, Docker/systemd deployment, session key generation, and on-chain registration.

candidates.title: Run a Parachain RPC Node on Polkadot
candidates.notes: Complete tutorial with Docker or systemd setup, chain spec download, snapshots, node launch, and sync monitoring.

candidates.title: Run an RPC Node for Polkadot Hub with Ethereum RPC Compatibility
candidates.notes: Complete tutorial covering Polkadot Hub RPC node setup via Docker/systemd, snapshot usage, and Ethereum RPC adapter setup.

candidates.title: Set Up a Polkadot Bootnode
candidates.notes: Guide for running a bootnode with P2P/WS/WSS connections, nginx proxy, and testing. Assumes a working polkadot binary and nginx.

candidates.title: Set Up and Run a Polkadot Full Node
candidates.notes: Complete tutorial covering binary installation (macOS/Windows/Linux/Docker/snap), node configuration, RPC settings, syncing, and Polkadot.js Apps connection.

candidates.title: Set Up Secure WebSocket for a Polkadot Node
candidates.notes: Guide for securing a node's WebSocket port with SSL using nginx or Apache2 proxy. Assumes a running node already exists.

candidates.title: Manage Polkadot Validator Session and Node Keys
candidates.notes: Procedural guide for generating and submitting session keys. Part of the multi-page validator setup flow; not standalone.

candidates.title: Set Up a Polkadot Validator Node
candidates.notes: Core composite tutorial spanning 3 pages: binary installation, key management, and activation. Produces an active validator on Polkadot.

candidates.title: Stop Validating on Polkadot
candidates.notes: Short guide covering chilling, purging session keys, and unbonding tokens. Brief, mostly Polkadot.js Apps UI actions.

candidates.title: Configure Monitoring and Optimize a Polkadot Validator
candidates.notes: Comprehensive tutorial covering CPU optimization, Prometheus, Grafana setup, Alertmanager with email alerts, and security hardening.

candidates.title: Set Up a Staking Operator Proxy on Polkadot
candidates.notes: Guide for setting up a Staking Operator proxy for non-custodial validator operations via Polkadot Hub.

candidates.title: Upgrade a Polkadot Validator Node
candidates.notes: Guide for upgrading a validator using a backup node (Validator A/B swap process). Requires existing validator setup.

candidates.title: Add an Existing Pallet to the Runtime
candidates.notes: Complete tutorial with numbered steps, code examples, cargo build, chain spec generation, local testing. CI tested.

candidates.title: Add Multiple Pallet Instances to Runtime
candidates.notes: Complete tutorial for adding multiple instances of pallet-collective. CI tested.

candidates.title: Create a Custom Pallet from Scratch
candidates.notes: Complete tutorial for creating a counter pallet with storage, events, errors, genesis config, and dispatchable functions. CI tested.

candidates.title: Create a Mock Runtime for Pallet Testing
candidates.notes: Creates mock.rs with construct_runtime!, frame_system config, genesis storage helpers. Depends on create-a-pallet. CI tested.

candidates.title: Write Unit Tests for a Custom Pallet
candidates.notes: Full test suite with 15 passing tests covering assert_ok!, assert_noop!, event testing, genesis config, access control. CI tested.

candidates.title: Benchmark a Custom Pallet
candidates.notes: Creates benchmarking module, generates weight files, updates runtime config. Produces production-ready weights.rs. CI tested.

candidates.title: Install Polkadot SDK Dependencies and Build
candidates.notes: Complete installation tutorial covering macOS, Linux, Windows WSL. Rust setup, SDK clone and build. CI tested.

candidates.title: Open HRMP Channels Between Parachains
candidates.notes: Step-by-step tutorial for opening unidirectional HRMP channel. Uses Polkadot.js Apps UI. Requires multi-parachain network setup. CI tested.

candidates.title: Open HRMP Channel with System Parachains
candidates.notes: Simpler than between-parachains (single XCM message for bidirectional channel). Requires relay chain + parachain + system chain. CI tested.

candidates.title: Deploy a Parachain to Polkadot TestNet
candidates.notes: Comprehensive tutorial: account setup, parachain ID reservation, key generation, chain spec config, parathread registration, collator startup.

candidates.title: Obtain Coretime for Parachain Block Production
candidates.notes: Tutorial covering on-demand and bulk coretime via RegionX marketplace. Depends on deploy-to-polkadot.

candidates.title: Set Up and Run the Polkadot SDK Parachain Template
candidates.notes: Complete beginner tutorial: clone template, install tools, compile runtime, generate chain spec, run locally. CI tested.

candidates.title: Renew Coretime for Parachain Operation
candidates.notes: Manual renewal via broker.renew and auto-renewal via XCM. Requires existing coretime purchase and HRMP channel setup.

candidates.title: Perform a Forkless Runtime Upgrade
candidates.notes: Adds new dispatchable, bumps spec_version, builds Wasm, submits upgrade via sudo. CI tested.

candidates.title: Implement Storage Migrations for Runtime Upgrades
candidates.notes: Covers migration scenarios, OnRuntimeUpgrade trait, VersionedMigration pattern, testing hooks. Has code examples but no end-to-end runnable tutorial.

candidates.title: Unlock a Parachain via XCM
candidates.notes: Guide for checking lock status and sending XCM to relay chain to remove lock. Requires existing deployed parachain with sudo.

candidates.title: Fork a Live Parachain Using Chopsticks
candidates.notes: Complete tutorial with Chopsticks installation, YAML config, CLI flags, and WebSocket commands for chain manipulation. CI tested.

candidates.title: Run a Parachain Test Network Using Zombienet
candidates.notes: Building parachain binary, downloading relay chain binaries, TOML config, spawning network, verifying block production. CI tested.

candidates.title: Fork and Test Polkadot SDK Chains Locally with Chopsticks
candidates.notes: Installation, configuration, forking chains, replaying blocks, and testing XCM. Clear procedural content with concrete outcomes.

candidates.title: Interact with Polkadot Chains Using Dedot JavaScript Client
candidates.notes: Installation, client initialization, reading on-chain data, signing/sending transactions.

candidates.title: Set Up End-to-End Tests for Polkadot SDK Chains with Moonwall
candidates.notes: Installation, initialization, configuration, writing tests, running tests. Produces a working E2E test suite.

candidates.title: Run a Parachain Node Using Polkadot Omni Node
candidates.notes: Install binary, obtain chain specs, run a full node, interact with it. Clear procedural content with tangible outcome.

candidates.title: Interact with Polkadot Chains Using Polkadot-API (PAPI)
candidates.notes: Installation, API instantiation with multiple providers, reading chain data, sending transactions.

candidates.title: Interact with Polkadot Chains Using Polkadart Dart SDK
candidates.notes: Installation, type generation, API instance creation, reading data, sending transactions in Dart. Niche audience.

candidates.title: Interact with Polkadot Chains Using Polkadot.js API
candidates.notes: Installation, API instance, chain data, transactions. In maintenance mode; lower priority due to deprecation.

candidates.title: Bootstrap and Run a Parachain Project with Pop CLI
candidates.notes: Install Pop CLI, set up dev environment, scaffold a new parachain project, build it, launch local network.

candidates.title: Interact with Polkadot Chains Using Python Substrate Interface
candidates.notes: Installation, connection, reading chain state, submitting transactions in Python.

candidates.title: Set Up and Use Substrate Sidecar REST API
candidates.notes: Installation, connecting to local/remote node, querying endpoints. Produces a running REST API service.

candidates.title: Interact with Polkadot Chains Using Subxt Rust Library
candidates.notes: Install dependencies, download metadata, generate types, read data, submit transactions in Rust.

candidates.title: Deploy Ephemeral Test Networks with Zombienet
candidates.notes: Installation, provider config, network config, spawning networks, writing/running tests.

candidates.title: Deploy a Basic Contract with Remix IDE
candidates.notes: Step-by-step tutorial to compile and deploy a basic Storage contract to Polkadot Hub TestNet using Remix IDE.

candidates.title: Deploy an ERC-20 Token Using Remix IDE
candidates.notes: Complete tutorial to create, compile, deploy, and interact with an ERC-20 token using Remix IDE and OpenZeppelin.

candidates.title: Deploy an ERC-721 NFT Using Hardhat
candidates.notes: Complete tutorial with project setup, Hardhat config, contract creation, compilation, and deployment of an ERC-721 NFT.

candidates.title: Deploy an ERC-721 NFT Using Remix IDE
candidates.notes: Step-by-step tutorial to create, compile, and deploy an ERC-721 NFT contract using Remix IDE and OpenZeppelin.

candidates.title: Set Up Foundry and Deploy a Contract on Polkadot Hub
candidates.notes: Comprehensive tutorial covering Foundry installation, project init, compilation, deployment, verification, and interaction.

candidates.title: Set Up Hardhat Polkadot for PVM Development
candidates.notes: Step-by-step tutorial for initializing a Hardhat project with the Polkadot PVM plugin, compiling to PVM bytecode, testing locally, and deploying.

candidates.title: Connect Remix IDE to Polkadot Hub
candidates.notes: Short guide on accessing Remix IDE and connecting via MetaMask. Setup prerequisite for Remix-based tutorials.

candidates.title: Get Test Tokens from the Polkadot Faucet
candidates.notes: Brief 3-step guide for obtaining PAS test tokens. Simple prerequisite action, not a standalone skill.

candidates.title: Configure a Wallet for Polkadot Hub
candidates.notes: Guide to connect MetaMask and Talisman to Polkadot Hub TestNet. Prerequisite setup.

candidates.title: Deploy and Interact with a Contract Using Ethers.js
candidates.notes: Full tutorial: project setup, provider config, contract compilation, deployment, and interaction using Ethers.js.

candidates.title: Deploy and Interact with a Contract Using viem
candidates.notes: Full tutorial: TypeScript project setup, chain config, client/wallet setup, contract compilation, deployment, and interaction.

candidates.title: Build a dApp Frontend with Wagmi and React
candidates.notes: Tutorial to set up a Next.js project with Wagmi v3, connect wallets, fetch blockchain data, and interact with a deployed contract.

candidates.title: Deploy and Interact with a Contract Using Web3.js
candidates.notes: Full tutorial for Web3.js project setup, compilation, deployment, and interaction. Web3.js is sunset, reducing priority.

candidates.title: Deploy and Interact with a Contract Using Web3.py
candidates.notes: Full Python tutorial: project setup, contract compilation with py-solc-x, deployment, and interaction.

candidates.title: Interact with the XCM Precompile via Remix
candidates.notes: Guide for using XCM precompile for cross-chain messaging from smart contracts via Remix. Includes weighing, executing, and sending XCM messages.

supplementary_only.notes: Glossary of terms and definitions for the Polkadot ecosystem
Documents account data structures, balance types, reference counters, and SS58 address formats
Documents weight system, fee calculation formulas, dispatch classes, and custom weight annotations
Documents transaction types, formats, lifecycle, signed extensions, and construction
Documents runtime metadata, RPC APIs, and how to retrieve/use chain data
Documents SCALE codec encoding/decoding traits, data type encodings, and available libraries
Documents available Polkadot networks (MainNet, Paseo, Kusama, Westend) and development path
Documents asset types, structure, operations, and roles/permissions on Polkadot Hub
Documents Bridge Hub architecture, pallets, trustless bridging mechanisms, and deployed bridges
Overview of ParaSpell XCM SDK suite. Links to separate tutorials for actual usage.
Overview of XCM tool collections (Moonsong Labs, ParaSpell, Astar, Chopsticks, Moonbeam SDK)
Lists network details (chain IDs, RPC URLs, explorer URLs) and faucet steps
Lists available block explorers (BlockScout, Routescan, Subscan) with links
Explains Ethereum-to-Polkadot address mapping, account types, and account registration
Explains block structure, EVM transaction types, and fee components
Compares REVM and PVM deployment models, factory patterns, two-step deployment
Explains Polkadot's dual VM architecture (REVM vs PVM) and when to choose each backend
Deep technical comparison of EVM and PVM: architecture, gas model, memory management, YUL IR translation
Explains gas estimation, weight-to-gas mapping, dynamic pricing on Polkadot Hub
Documents supported Ethereum JSON-RPC methods with parameters and curl examples
Documents the ERC20 precompile interface, query functions, token operations, and common asset IDs
Documents standard Ethereum-native precompiles (ECRecover, SHA-256, etc.) available on Polkadot Hub
Documents the Storage precompile interface with read/write/inspect functions
Documents the System precompile interface: cryptographic operations, account management, runtime queries
Documents hardware requirements, VPS providers, minimum bond (10,000 DOT), and minimum commission (10%)
Explains the chill mechanism for validators and nominators, staking election timing
Explains offense types, penalty levels, slash calculation formulas, and avoidance best practices
Explains era points, reward variance, payout scheme, and nominator/validator payment splits
Explains three approaches to adding smart contracts (pallet-revive, Frontier, pallet-contracts) without step-by-step implementation
Introduces XCM concepts: four principles, tech stack, core functionalities
Overview of smart contract support on Polkadot Hub via REVM and PVM
Explains light client concepts and workflow. Brief tool mentions but no complete procedural workflow.
